Event: Annie decides not to confess her love to Troy while he’s on a date
Explanation: She protects Troy’s current relationship and preserves their friendship while sparing herself likely rejection.

Event: Annie’s mother pushes her hard to succeed while belittling her
Explanation: Mother gains ego/validation from Annie’s performance while seriously harming Annie’s self-esteem.

Event: Annie repeats her mother’s harsh “commitment” speech to Pierce
Explanation: She reinforces her own work-ethic narrative but harshly belittles Pierce to motivate him.

Event: Annie lets her rivalry with Annie Kim turn Model UN into a “prestige war”
Explanation: Her status drive may boost her pride if she wins but makes the academic exercise hostile and petty for others.

Event: Annie slams Jeff’s head into a table
Explanation: She asserts herself physically (short-term catharsis, maybe respect) while clearly injuring Jeff.

Event: Annie punches Jeff so hard he bleeds
Explanation: Again she vents anger and asserts boundaries at the cost of physically harming a group member.

Event: Jeff’s burst of love for Annie unlocks the lab and saves Greendale
Explanation: His genuine love for Annie frees everyone and prevents the school’s sale while giving him emotional clarity.

Event: Annie repeatedly falls back into attraction to Jeff despite knowing it’s unhealthy
Explanation: Her pattern harms her own emotional stability while sometimes nudging Jeff toward growth and vulnerability.

Event: Jeff and Annie end tense moments with head pats / chin-chucks to defuse things
Explanation: These gestures maintain closeness and de-escalate conflict for both.

Event: Vaughn calls Annie “mountain flower” while dating her
Explanation: The affectionate nickname strengthens their bond without evident downside.

Event: Annie and Jeff use “milady/milord” as playful nicknames
Explanation: The shared language deepens rapport and mutual affection.

Event: Annie decides to get over her unrequited crush on Troy
Explanation: She moves on from a one-sided attachment, improving her own emotional health and easing things for Troy.

Event: Annie maintains emotional labor for a self-sabotaging study group
Explanation: She helps keep them together and improving but overextends and stresses herself.

Event: Annie chloroforms a janitor twice to cover the group’s misdeeds
Explanation: She protects herself/the group from consequences by seriously violating the janitor’s bodily autonomy.

Event: Annie almost chloroforms Jeff to manage the situation
Explanation: She’d prevent exposure for herself by harming Jeff, with little benefit to him.

Event: Annie jumps on a large man’s back and chokes him in “Analysis of Cork-Based Networking”
Explanation: She physically subdues a much bigger man to advance her agenda, helping her cause while endangering him.

Event: Annie seduces the elf maiden as Hector the Well Endowed during D&D
Explanation: Within the game she solves a problem creatively and entertains the group without real-world harm.

Event: Annie repeatedly uses her “Disney face” to sway the group
Explanation: She secures leniency and outcomes she wants by emotionally manipulating others at some cost to their resolve/autonomy.

Event: Jeff orders everyone to close their eyes so Annie’s tears don’t weaken them
Explanation: He protects their collective stance while still acknowledging Annie’s power to sway them.

Event: Annie aggressively whales on multiple opponents in “Comparative Religion” fight
Explanation: She proves her toughness and helps her friends in a brawl but does so by inflicting disproportionate violence.

Event: Troy only notices Annie’s attractiveness after Jeff points it out
Explanation: He gains awareness and may treat her differently; no clear cost or benefit to others beyond recognition.

Event: Jeff warns the group not to look at crying Annie because it will weaken their anger
Explanation: He helps them maintain justified anger and boundaries against Annie’s manipulative cuteness.

Event: Jeff repeatedly pushes Annie to avoid sexualizing herself early on
Explanation: He restrains his own impulses and pressures others not to objectify her, protecting her from premature sexualization.

Event: Annie uses her skills and planning to out-prepare academic/political opponents
Explanation: Her analytical work benefits her and often the institution, while only competitively pressuring rivals.

Event: Annie participates vigorously in campus paintball “wars” as a lethal player
Explanation: She excels at a consensual game, boosting her status and team success while only “harming” opponents in a playful context.

Event: Annie chooses not to sexualize herself until much later (initial modest dress, etc.)
Explanation: She protects her own comfort and image while not harming anyone else.

Event: Annie tells Jeff she thinks she can change him early on
Explanation: Her belief pressures Jeff toward growth, but sustaining unrealistic expectations costs her emotionally.

Event: Annie runs hard in student elections, treating them as “serious business”
Explanation: Her intensity improves organization and stakes, though it also ramps stress and conflict.

Event: Annie willingly becomes the dean’s overworked script supervisor in “Documentary Filmmaking: Redux”
Explanation: She sacrifices time and sanity to help the dean complete his commercial.

Event: Annie confronts Jeff at gunpoint over ignoring her after their kiss in “Conspiracy Theories”
Explanation: She forces an emotional reckoning with real risk, harming both trust and physical safety to satisfy hurt.

Event: Annie schemes to set Troy and Britta up to free Jeff for herself
Explanation: She manipulates their potential relationship largely to clear a path to Jeff, prioritizing her romantic interests over their autonomy.

Event: Annie ultimately chooses a DC/FBI career over a relationship with Jeff
Explanation: She advances her long-term career while giving Jeff freedom to grow, even though both feel pain.

Event: Jeff confesses his love and then lets Annie go pursue her career
Explanation: He gains honesty and emotional closure while supporting her better future rather than clinging.

Event: Annie dons a sexy Santa outfit to seduce Jeff into joining glee club
Explanation: She uses her sexuality to recruit him, benefiting the club and her goal while putting manipulative pressure on Jeff.

Event: Annie sneaks peeks at naked Jeff during the Coach Bogner showdown
Explanation: She gratifies her curiosity with minor invasion of his privacy.

Event: Annie obtains a fake ID to drink underage in “Mixology Certification”
Explanation: She risks legal and personal trouble for short-term adult-experience benefits; others are minimally affected.

Event: Annie carries a gun due to living in a dangerous neighborhood
Explanation: She improves her own safety and deterrence in a high-risk environment, at most threatening would-be aggressors.

Event: Annie moves in with Troy and Abed to fix her housing situation
Explanation: She gains safer housing and closeness; they gain a balancing roommate who helps structure their lives.

Event: Annie snaps at Troy and Abed about always having to adapt to their quirks
Explanation: Her outburst leads to a healthier compromise where they also accommodate her needs.

Event: Annie helps run the “Save Greendale” committee with heavy organizational labor
Explanation: She overworks herself to stabilize and improve the school, clearly benefiting others more than herself.

Event: Annie investigates scam courses with Jeff in “Conspiracy Theories and Interior Design”
Explanation: She uncovers fraud and enforces standards, improving institutional integrity while only inconveniencing wrongdoers.

Event: Annie aggressively interrogates the group and strips everyone for the missing pen
Explanation: Her obsession yields no resolution, humiliates friends, and damages trust without benefiting herself.

Event: Annie repeatedly joins in bullying Todd whenever he appears
Explanation: She gains in-group acceptance and humor at the cost of Todd’s well-being.

Event: Annie gasps and plays up cute indignation to influence others’ reactions
Explanation: She often secures sympathy or leverage by exaggerating innocence, subtly manipulating others.

Event: Annie uses her “Puppy-Dog Eyes” to get Jeff to do the right thing
Explanation: She tugs his conscience in a slightly manipulative way but reliably pushes him toward better behavior.

Event: Jeff refrains from fully pursuing Annie because of their age gap
Explanation: He sacrifices a desired relationship to protect her from a problematic dynamic and his own immaturity.

Event: Annie enables Jeff’s emotional dependence by being his “living emotional crutch”
Explanation: She stabilizes and improves Jeff but keeps herself tethered to his issues, limiting her own growth.

Event: Annie chooses to stop pursuing Jeff in Season 5 when she thinks he’ll marry Britta
Explanation: She accepts his apparent choice, sparing herself more pain and giving him space to be “happy,” even if misinformed.

Event: Annie uses lists, binders, and strict scheduling to control group projects
Explanation: Her organization makes tasks feasible and successful, though sometimes at the cost of others’ comfort with flexibility.

Event: Annie helps form and run Jeff’s student election campaign
Explanation: She boosts Jeff’s chances and campaign quality, improving process seriousness more than harming any rival.

Event: Annie uses her sexuality (cleavage, flirting) to gain leverage over Jeff in “Debate 109”
Explanation: She secures attention and advantage in the moment while putting pressure on Jeff via seduction rather than argument alone.

Event: Annie and Jeff share a “Big Damn Kiss” in the S1 finale
Explanation: They both gain emotional and romantic fulfillment, though it complicates things later without clear present harm.

Event: Jeff ignores Annie all summer after the S1 kiss
Explanation: He avoids emotional discomfort for himself while deeply hurting and confusing her.

Event: Annie calling herself “Irony-Free Annie” and continuing to invest idealistically
Explanation: Her sincerity often motivates better behavior and cohesion in the group while giving her a meaningful role.
